mission: Move setup wizard guidance from top-level `allowedTools` to `permissions.allow` (issue #197). Root cause: when `allowedTools` is present in project `.claude/settings.json`, Claude Code silently disables its auto-mode classifier — same failure family as #198's model pin. Setup Step 9 now writes `permissions.allow` with an explicit anti-`allowedTools` warning. Update skill adds Step 7.6 migration with four branches; byte-for-byte preservation when migrating.
success: Reviewer verifies round-1 finding #1 is resolved: Step 7.6 'both present' branch no longer contains contradictory dedup/preserve language. It now says 'append ... preserving order ... Do NOT dedup' matching the byte-for-byte rule. A new regression test at tests/test-cli.sh:test_update_skill_migration_is_non_destructive gates this. Also re-verifies all round-1 passes (a, c, d, e, f, g, h) still hold.
failure: Reviewer misses that the 'append' rewrite has a subtle data-loss path if permissions.allow already contains an entry like `Bash(git *)` and allowedTools has `Bash(git:*)` — the appended copy stays but Claude Code might treat one as a shadow of the other. Reviewer doesn't sanity-check the negative control. Reviewer rubber-stamps the regression test without reading whether its regex actually rejects the anti-pattern.
fixes_applied: Finding 1 (P1): skills/update/SKILL.md Step 7.6 — 'both present' branch rewritten. Replaced 'union the two lists (dedup), drop allowedTools' with 'append every entry from allowedTools to the end of permissions.allow (preserving order within each list), then drop the legacy allowedTools key. Do NOT dedup.' Explicit rationale: duplicate entries might be intentional, and silent dedup violates the byte-for-byte preservation rule. Added tests/test-cli.sh:test_update_skill_migration_is_non_destructive as a regression guard. 70/70 test-cli.sh pass.
verification_checklist: (a) Read skills/update/SKILL.md Step 7.6 'both present' branch — confirm the new wording says 'append ... preserving order ... Do NOT dedup' and does NOT say 'union ... dedup' or 'dedup the list'.
(b) Verify the rewrite is internally consistent with the byte-for-byte rule at the end of Step 7.6. The 'both present' branch and the overall rule should not contradict.

(c) Read tests/test-cli.sh:test_update_skill_migration_is_non_destructive — walk through the three grep conditions. Confirm they collectively gate the regression: positive migration language with 'dedup' is rejected.
(d) Negative control yourself: reintroduce 'union the two lists (dedup)' into Step 7.6, rerun tests/test-cli.sh, confirm test_update_skill_migration_is_non_destructive fails. Restore.
(e) Run `bash tests/test-cli.sh`. Confirm 70/70 pass (was 69/69, +1 new test).
(f) Re-verify round-1 passes still hold: Step 9 recommends permissions.allow, no leftover live allowedTools guidance, merge-compat tests still pass.
review_instructions: TARGETED RECHECK — not a full re-review. Verify the fix to finding #1 resolves the contradiction and the new test actually gates the regression. Evaluate if the 'append, no dedup' instruction is the right call (it is — dedup would silently remove user entries, which violates the mission's byte-for-byte rule). Do NOT raise new findings unless P0.
